title: The One With the Blind Dates

lines.text: Joey and Rachel's apartment. Rachel is in the kitchen as Joey enters from his bedroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Morning, roomie!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! You remembered to put clothes on this morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fifth day's a charm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Joey, it's so great to be back here. I gotta tell you, you're making it so easy on me and Emma.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, it's great having you back. You know, stay as long as you want, and when does she stop crying all night?
Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you're not naked! So hey, Rach, when will we expect to see you tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'll probably be back to pick her up around six, but she's in the bedroom all ready to go. But she did actually fall back to sleep, so...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She's probably exhausted from all that adorable screaming she did last night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye!
She leaves.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye! Hey, I hope Emma isn't making it too hard on you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, hey, it's been great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. And look, I just want you to know that with Rachel staying here and everything, all my feelings from before are totally over, okay? And even if they weren't, when you accidentally walk in on a woman using a breast pump...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, that'll do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! So, how are you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm, I'm okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, I mean, do I wish me and Rachel living together would have worked out? Of course. You know, I'm disappointed, but it's not like it's a divorce.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, actually it...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it's not a divorce, it is not a divorce! Anyway, I think Rachel and I need to, you know, get on with our lives, maybe, maybe start seeing other people.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow, really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, sure, why not? In fact, if you know anyone that would be good for me...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure, I know lots of girls.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Any names come to mind?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, names?
Opening credits.
Joey and Rachel's apartment. Joey is there as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. I was just gonna get something to eat. You want something?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What you got?
Joey (checks the refrigerator): Okay, let's see, we got strained peas, strained carrots... Ooh! Strained plums. We haven't tried that yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Goodie! Thanks. So, how is it living with Rachel again? I mean, apart from the great food.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm fine, I'm fine, it's just, it's just weird what's happening with her and Ross. You know, yesterday he asked me to fix him up with somebody.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my god, Rachel asked me if I knew anyone for her too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why are they doing this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know. They're so perfect for each other; it's crazy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know what's crazy? These jars. What is it, like two bites in here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just wish they'd realise they should be together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, I know. And when they moved back in together, I figured y'know, that's where things were headed.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. They should be a family. They should get married and have more children.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, and they should name one of their kids Joey. I may not have kids; someone's gotta carry on the family name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know what? Maybe once they start dating, and they see what's out there, they'll realise how good they are for each other.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, because it is slim pickings. I had this date last night: Yuck! But we should probably keep it down; she's still in the bedroom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, what are we gonna do? Are we just gonna go ahead and set them up with people?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know; that just pushes them further and further apart.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. (BEAT) Oh, I know what we can do. We could set Ross and Rachel up on horrible dates, so that they'll realise how good they are together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, that's a great plan!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you know what the best part of it is? I get to do my "plan-laugh." (A maniacal laugh follows.)
They both start laughing really loudly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Shhh! Not so loud, we don't wanna wake up, uh...
He looks at his bedroom door, but he can't remember the name of the girl.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. They're in the kitchen as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys aren't doing anything tonight, are you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, now, why would you assume that? Just because we're married? I will have you know that we are very hip, happening people. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have to get back to reading the obituaries.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I was just asking 'cause I need someone to watch Emma tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure, we'll do that. What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Phoebe set me up on a date.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? What's the big deal?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just figured, 'cause you and Ross are...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, slept together a year and a half ago? Yeah, I'm all set.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I think it's great that you're going on a date, you know? I mean, it sounds healthy. I mean, you have needs. Embrace your womanhood!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You want a job? Turn off "Oprah," and send out a resume!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So I'll bring her by around seven? Is that okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, it's perfect.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you guys are gonna have so much fun! She's at such a cute age. Oh, a couple things. Now that she's eating solid food, she poops around the clock. And watch out for your hair, 'cause she likes to grab it. And oh, she's also in this phase where if you leave the room, she screams bloody murder, but ah... Thanks, you guys. Have fun!
Chandler (reading the newspaper): Suddenly I wish I was reading my own name.
Central Perk. Joey is drinking coffee by the counter as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. I'm so excited; I just set up Rachel with the worst guy tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Who is he?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it's this guy I used to massage. And by massage, I mean hold down so he wouldn't turn over and flash me.
Joey (gives a thumbs up sign): Okay, okay. Wait till you hear who I got for Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah.
They sit down on the couch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She's this really boring woman. She's a teacher!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A teacher?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, she's really into history and foreign movies... And oh, oh, she loves puzzles. Huh? Come on, who loves puzzles?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, Ross does. What... You're - you're ruining the plan! Joey, you've - you've fixed him up with his perfect woman!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my god, you're right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She even reads for pleasure!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How do you even know a woman like that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? I'm not allowed to know smart women?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I met her at the library. I went in to pee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So now what do we do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, okay, I'll - I'll just call her and tell her the date's cancelled, and find him somebody else.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What if we don't find him somebody else? We'll just tell her the date's off, but we don't tell Ross, and he goes to the restaurant and gets stood up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh...I hear that's bad.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, so this is great! Rachel's gonna have a terrible date, Ross gets stood up, and then they'll realise how good they have it together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, yes, The Plan! (Laughs loudly again, but he sounds more like Santa Claus.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's not Santa's plan. No, it's... (Laughs the real "plan-laugh.")
They both start laughing again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you know, it's not that fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I think we killed it.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Emma is there in her playpen, while Chandler is behind the couch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Emma? Emma? Look at me! Well, I think I'll go downstairs for a while.
He does the ancient trick of going downstairs while behind the couch. As soon as he's out of sight, Emma starts crying.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no, no! No, no, no, no! It's okay, it's okay. I didn't go. Don't cry, it's just a bit! I'm your uncle Chandler; funny is all I have!
Monica enters from their bedroom with a calendar.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, just so you know, I'm gonna be ovulating from tomorrow until the sixth, so don't touch yourself in the next 48 hours.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't do that.
Monica looks at him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll try to stop. Wait, did you say until the sixth?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Today is the sixth.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it's not.
Points at the calendar.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, it's also 2003.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god. Today's the sixth?! I may be done ovulating! I may have also served some very questionable meat at the restaurant.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's okay. Go take the test and see if we're okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
She runs to the bathroom, while Chandler starts acting like a chicken in front of Emma. Emma is silent, however.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tough crib.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, where are all my ovulation-sticks? There's only one here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I might have checked to see if I was ovulating a couple times.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am not working. There's not much to do around here!
Delmonico's restaurant. Ross is waiting for his blind date to show up. A waiter walks past him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me, is there a woman waiting at the bar? Someone average height, dark hair, perhaps doing a puzzle?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Uh, there's a drunk Chinese guy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, if I'm still here in an hour, buy him a drink on me.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Can I get you another glass of wine?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nah, I don't know if I should. I don't wanna be drunk when I go home alone.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Got stood up, huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, it's no big deal. It's just a blind date.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Are you worried your date came, saw you, and left?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!
The waiter leaves.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica emerges from the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're okay. I'm still ovulating.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, good, because as of four o'clock this afternoon, I am not.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, let's do this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I - I don't think I can.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on. I know you're not eighteen anymore, but give it a minute.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because of Emma.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, Emma. Oh, sweetie, I forgot you were here. Oh, you're right, we can't do this. We can't leave her alone.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Unless... Maybe we do it here. I mean, how much can she even be aware of at this age?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, she's aware when we leave the room. She may notice if we start... canoodling in it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Canoodling?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I can't say "hump" or "screw" in front of the B-A-B-Y.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know. I mean, I guess having sex in front of a baby isn't so...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Horrifying? Scarring? Something people go to jail for?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess you're right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You guess I'm right? When we stayed at that bed and breakfast, you wouldn't have sex with me because you thought a deer was staring through the window.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But what kind of a sick bastard wants to do it in front of a deer?
Another restaurant. Rachel is studying the menu together with her date, Steve. Steve is the stoned restaurateur from 115 TOW the Stoned Guy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow, everything looks so good! I think I'm gonna have the chicken.
Steve (staring at Rachel): I - I just have to say this; you're really beautiful.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, that's - that's very sweet. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: I'm kind of funny looking.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Oh, come on, you're way out of my league. Everybody in here knows it. Bet that guy over there's probably saying, "ooh, why she out with him? He must be rich!" Well, I'm not!
Rachel (feeling awkward): So, what do think you wanna order? I'm really excited about that chicken.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: I'm not funny either. So, if you were thinking, "well, he's not that good-looking, but maybe we'll have some laughs"... That ain't gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, come on, Steve; let's not rule out nervous laughter. Hey, now wait a minute. Phoebe told me that - that you owned your own restaurant. That's impressive.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: I lost it. To drugs.
Steve makes a face as if his mouth is too dry.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: I silk-screen t-shirts now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really? What's that like?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: It's really fulfilling doing something you hate for no money. That's right. I have no money, I'm not funny, I live in a studio apartment with two other guys, and I'm pretty sure I'm infertile.
Rachel (awkward chuckle): Now, come on, come on, Steve. There must be something that you like about yourself.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: I do like my hair.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?
Central Perk. Phoebe and Joey are there. Phoebe's cell phone rings.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello?
Rachel is still at the restaurant, but Steve is gone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, it's me. I'm going to hunt you down and kill you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is the worst date ever. How could you set me up with this creep?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know, you are talking about one of my dear, dear friends.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care! This guy is a nightmare!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, right, so he gets a little crazy when he's stoned.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He's not stoned.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Did he go out for a cigarette?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, four times.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My dear, sweet Rach.
Rachel hangs up in disgust.
Phoebe (to Joey): Well, our plan is working. Rachel is having a miserable time, and Ross is just stood up somewhere at a restaurant all alone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, great, pretty soon they'll be back together.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: By the time anyone's figured out what we've done, we'll be in sunny Mexico. (BEAT) Oh, wait, that's the end of a different plan.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Emma has fallen asleep in her playpen, and Chandler has fallen asleep right next to her on the floor. He's even sucking on a pacifier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's asleep. Chandler?
Chandler wakes up and looks a bit confused when he finds that he has a pacifier in his mouth.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Emma was doing it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's asleep.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, she's asleep, that means we can...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, but we have to be fast.
Chandler (laughs): Okay, I'll try. And you can't make any noise.
Monica (laughs): Okay, I'll try.
They run to the bedroom and close the door carefully just as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello?
Emma starts making noises, and Joey walks over to her playpen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Emma? Hey! Hi!
He picks her up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How are ya? How are ya? Where are your babysitters, huh? Why's the bedroom door closed?
He walks over, but just before he knocks on the door, he hears some moans and looks shocked.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You can't have S-E-X, when you're taking care of the B-A-B-I-E!
He walks out quickly with Emma in his arms.
Delmonico's. Ross is still waiting for his date and drinking wine. The waiter comes up to him again.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: I've got bad news. The Chinese guy left.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh, if it was meant to be, it's meant to be.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Look; you got stood up, who cares? We're gonna show you a good time. Just sit and relax. In fact, let me bring you a crab cake appetizer on the house.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, free crab cakes. Well, that's nice. Although I was hoping to have sex tonight.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Ooh...
Ross sees the waiter looking at him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just the crab cakes.
Meanwhile, another waiter has come up to the first waiter.
Waiter #2: What are you doing? Are you trying to get him to stay? Because you can't do that.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Just get out of here, okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's - what's going on?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Eh, okay, the waiters have a little pool going. We have a bet on how long it'll take before you give up and go home.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? You - you're making money off my misery?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Well, if you stay till 9:20, I am.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is unbelievable. I - I have never been so insulted in my life. Now, if you'll wrap up my free crab cakes, I'll be on my way.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. They're done and come out of their bedroom. Emma is still gone.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, that was weird. You were loud, and I was fast.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think we may have really done it this time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I wish I didn't have to wait to take a pregnancy test.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You may wanna get some more of those too.
They walk over to the playpen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where's Emma?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, where's Emma? Where's Emma?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't ask me, I was in there canoodling you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'm sure that Rachel came home early and picked up Emma. You go look across the hall, and I'll call her cell.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (Runs out.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, you better hope that we're pregnant, because one way or another, we're giving a baby back to Rachel.
The street in front of Central Perk. Rachel and Steve are walking home from their date. Steve is sobbing.
Steve (sobbing): I - I can't believe I’m crying in front of you. You must think I'm so pathetic.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, no, I admire a man who can cry.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Really?
He puts his hand on her shoulder.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't touch my coat!
Her cell phone rings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, sorry, it's my phone. Hello?
Monica (on phone): Hey, Rach, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my god, this is the worst date ever!
Steve starts crying loudly.
Rachel (to Steve): Look, you know what, I'm sorry, but did you really think that this was going well? (To Monica.) What's up?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, did you stop by here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, then...
Joey and Chandler enter with Emma.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, thank god! Emma, there you are!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What do you mean, "there you are"? Where was she?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, we were playing "peek-a-boo." She just ?she loves it when I'm dramatic.
Monica hangs up, and Rachel looks at her phone.
Monica (to Joey): Why the hell did you take her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because you two were having sex!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, we weren't!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't you lie to me! I could tell by Chandler's hair. (To Chandler.) You are so lazy. Can't you get on top for once?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, all right, we were. We were trying to make a baby. Monica's ovulating.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! It is unacceptable that you two would have sex with Emma in the next room. I'm gonna have to tell Rachel about this.
Joey starts to leave.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, please don't. Please, Joey. She will kill us!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I gotta! Unless...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Unless what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Unless you name your firstborn child Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I may never have kids, and somebody's gotta carry on my family name.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Your family name is Tribbiani.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (BEAT) (Laughs.) You almost had me.
He leaves.
The street in front of Central Perk. Steve and Rachel are still there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, uh...

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Look, I think I know the answer to this question, but... Would you like to make love to me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really, really not.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Eh, it's just as well. Doesn't work anyway.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, well that's good to know. Good night, Steve.
She walks over to Central Perk and enters to find Ross sitting on the couch, eating crab cakes. She takes off her coat while groaning and shuddering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what's wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just had a rough night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Crab cake?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Eww!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, what happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, I...It's kind of weird talking to you about this, but...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica told me you had a blind date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I did, too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But is it technically a date if the other person doesn't show up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, oh no. Do you think she walked in, saw you and left?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why does everyone keep saying that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, if it makes you feel any better, I wish my date hadn't shown up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That bad?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, he makes t-shirts for a living, and he thought it would be appropriate to give me this.
She holds up a black t-shirt with "FBI - Female Body Inspector" on the front.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Female body inspector? What size is that?
[Cut to outside. Phoebe and Joey are walking down the street to Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now, wait a minute. So, they're gonna name their first child Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How - how do I get them to name the next one after me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's easy, you just walk in on them having sex.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, so they owe me like, three Phoebes.
Phoebe sees Rachel and Ross through the window.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my god! Look, it's Ross and Rachel. Oh, the plan is working.
Joey does the "plan-laugh."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't, don't do the plan-laugh.
[Cut to inside Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The first date we've had in months, and they were both such disasters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Huh. You know, it is weird that Phoebe would set me up on a date that was awful on the same night that Joey set you up on a date that didn't even show.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute; you don't think it was intentional? I mean, that's just stupid.
[Cut to outside Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We're geniuses! Yeah, look at them, look at them, they're really bonding.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, they're falling in love all over again.
Rachel and Ross turn around and look at Phoebe and Joey with puzzled expressions on their faces.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, they see us! Oh, they, they look mad. Oh, they figured it out. They're coming this way. Run!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mexico!
They run down the street with Ross and Rachel following right behind them.
End credits.
Delmonico's restaurant. Ross and Joey are sitting at a table for four. The waiter is pouring water in their glasses.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can you believe they're still not here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. A double blind date, and we both get stood up. What are the chances?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, I'm so bummed. Can we have our free crab cakes now?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We've been stood up. (sniffles) And we want our free crab cakes.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Guys, give it a rest. Nobody's betting on you tonight. Although we do have a pool going to see how long it takes that guy to cry.
He points at Steve who's sitting at another table. He's staring at his hands.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: I have such fat hands!
He starts crying.
